tables.name: entries
tables.label: Entries
tables.description: The universal node — a project, task, comment, note, or embedded tool (wireframe / sheet / doc / sketch). Every entry has a title and a comment; its type decides its content. Nodes DON'T store a parent — they relate to each other through the `relations` table, so one node can live under many parents.

tables.name: relations
tables.label: Relations
tables.description: The edges of the graph. Each row links two entries: {fromId, toId, type}. type `contains` is structural parent→child (builds the tree); references / depends_on / relates_to / blocks (and custom) are free cross-links. A node can be linked under many parents (many-to-many).

tables.name: users
tables.label: People
tables.description: The people on this board. Defined here, addable any time; an entry's 'created by' and 'prompting' chips point at them.
